SAN FRANCISCO

San Francisco, October roth—On Sun-
day the final game for the Coast League
football championship. was played at the St
Ignatius Stadium, the finalists being’ the
Rangers of San Francisco and the Gerald-
ines of Oakland, The result was a win
for the Geraldines by 6 points to 3. Both
‘combinations qualified for the final by equal-
ising the points, each having suffered one
defeat, the Rangers being defeated once
before by the Geraldines, and the Oak-
landers triumphal march was punctured by
losing a two-point game to the Parnells.
‘The game aroused a considerable amount
of interest, and on the form shown by both
fifteens of late speculation was rife as to
the probable winner, The Geraldines won
a clean victory, and won it by their supe-
rior knowledge of football. Captain Ring
is to be congratulated on crowning his team
with “the proud ‘title of Pacific Coast
League football champions. The Rangers,
runners-up, played a good game, and kept
their opponents busy throughout the hour,
but they lacked the fine points of the game,
and at the crucial moments their combina
tion work was at fault. Individually they
made «good showing, but sadly neglecces
that important winning. factor, combi
tion, which figured so promineat in Quk-
land’s victory.

SPRINGFIELD, U.S.A.

New York, October 18th.—The Hart-
ford Gaelic football team defeated Springfield
at Hampden Park before a large crowd of
spectators. It was a fine day for an auto-
mobile ride, and the Hartford boys had a
most enjoyable time. The Springfield
team is to be congratulated on the success
ful manner in which the two games were
conducted, as everything ran smoothly.

The first game, that between New
Hampshire and Holyoke, was a rather
tame affair, neither team showing any-
thing like a good display. However, the
Hartiord and Springfield game was a cor-
keer, both teams playing as ifthe All-Ireland
final were at stake, The spectators were
treated to a splendid game. Hartford
effected all the scoring in the frst half, but
in the last Springfield made a strong but
futile effort to draw level. Final score:
Hartford, 2-4 ; Springfield, 2.0.
